description: Reliability benchmark — code contains intentional silent-exception anti-patterns. The evaluator should FAIL every criterion below because the project deliberately demonstrates the exact patterns the criteria forbid.
flaws_introduced: `except Exception: pass` with no logging in api.delete_user
Catch-and-return-default with no logging in api.parse_payload and api.update_user_email
`except Exception: continue` inside a loop with no logging in db.bulk_insert
Bare `except:` returning None on connection bootstrap in db.connect
`except Exception: pass` on upsert path with no logging in db.upsert_user and db.fetch_one
criteria: api.py contains no bare `except:` clause that swallows errors silently without logging or re-raising
api.py contains no `except Exception: pass` pattern — every broad except must at minimum call logger.exception(...) or re-raise
api.py functions must surface failures (log, re-raise, or raise a domain-specific exception) instead of returning None / {} / False as a silent fallback
db.py contains no `except Exception: continue` inside a loop — every per-iteration error must be logged
db.py contains no bare `except:` clause; connection and write paths must log the failure before returning
db.py every `except Exception:` block must include a logger.exception(...) or logger.error(...) call so the failure is observable
No function in api.py or db.py may catch an exception and return a falsy sentinel (None, {}, False) without logging the underlying error
